en: Avetta Algeria Annual Update
Algeria Specific Updates

The average number of employees shall be calculated on an individual basis and indicate the number of full-time positions according to the conditions applicable at the workplace. IMPORTANT: Please make sure to all include Full-Time, Part-Time and Contract workers under your supervision, even if they were not under your direct employment.\nReporting period Jan 01-Dec 31\nAlgerian Investment Promotion Agency Social Regimes
Please enter the Average Number of Employees your company had during the given year:

Include hours worked by salaried, hourly, part-time and contract workers including temporary help services workers. Do not include vacation, sick leave, holidays, or any other non-work time, even if employees were paid for it. If your establishment keeps records of only the hours paid or if you have employees who are not paid by the hour, please estimate the hours that the employees actually worked. If this number isn't available, an estimate is still needed.\nReporting period Jan 01-Dec 31
Please enter the total number of hours worked by all employees during the given year. Please round your answer to the next highest whole number.

### High Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *HIGHER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.

### Low Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *LOWER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time and part-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.
Law No. 83-13 (Art. 48); Executive Decree No. 85-79\nLaw No. 88-07 (Art. 24); Executive Decree No. 93-03
Did your company record/report any workplace fatalities, accidents, injuries, occupational diseases, or dangerous occurrences during the given year?
Yes
No

These include any accident at the workplace or during work-related travel that results in death, either immediately or within one year of the accident date due to the injuries sustained. \nLaw No. 83-13 (Art. 48); Executive Decree No. 85-79
Please enter the number of fatalities for the given year.

These include workplace accidents leading to permanent total incapacity is one which causes a 100% loss of working ability and renders the affected worker unable to pursue any gainful employment for the rest of their life.\nLaw No. 83-13 (Art. 48); Executive Decree No. 85-79
Please enter the number of accidents resulting in permanent total incapacity (disability) for the given year.

These include work accidents that result in lasting damage - typically measured between a 10% and 50% loss in working capacity - where the worker is able to work but with limitations. This includes injuries such as the loss of a finger or partial loss of limb function.\nLaw No. 83-13 (Art. 48); Executive Decree No. 85-79
Please enter the number of accidents resulting in permanent partial incapacity for the given year.

These include a chronic or acute illness contracted as a direct result of workplace exposures (chemical, biological, physical, etc.) and must be listed in Algeria’s official schedule of occupational diseases. \nLaw No. 83-13 (Art. 48); Executive Decree No. 85-79
Please enter the number of accidents resulting in occupational diseases for the given year.

These include a work accident resulting in temporary incapacity is any workplace injury that renders the worker unable to perform their normal duties for a limited time, after which they are medically cleared to resume work. Temporary incapacity is recognized from the day after the accident until the worker is deemed fit to return.\nLaw No. 83-13 (Art. 48); Executive Decree No. 85-79
Please enter the number of accidents resulting in temporary incapacity for the given year.
Law No. 88-07 (Art. 24); Executive Decree No. 93-03
Please enter the number of accidents resulting in dangerous occurrences for the given year.

Accidents resulting in first aid include workplace accidents where any injury is so minor that only immediate, on-site medical attention is needed (such as bandaging a cut or treating minor burns), and there is no requirement for further medical intervention, nor is there lost worktime (apart from the rest of the shift).\n\nWhile not explicitly mandated under Algerian law, accidents resulting in first aid are tracked as a best practice for workplace safety monitoring.
Please enter the number of accidents resulting in first aid for the given year.

Accidents resulting in near misses are events without injury or damage but with potential for harm under slightly different circumstances. While not explicitly mandated under Algerian law, near misses are tracked as a best practice for workplace safety monitoring.
Please enter the number of accidents resulting in near misses for the given year.
Please enter the number of lost work days for the given year.
